semanticCore: Orphanhood, singularity, loss of parents, or fatigue
quranicRelevance: Orphans (Yateem/Yatama) who lost their fathers before maturity, and protecting their rights.
figurativeOrRareUsages: يَتِيمًا ذَا مَقْرَبَةٍ (90:15): An orphan of near relationship (urging special care).
discardedNoise: Obscure details on fatigue (يتم) in horses.
summaryEn: The root denotes orphanhood and vulnerability, focusing on the ethical and financial protection of parentless children (Yatama).
summaryAr: يدل هذا الجذر على اليتم والانفراد، مركزاً على الحماية الأخلاقية والمالية للأطفال فاقدي الآباء (يتامى).
rawText: أَيْتَمَهُ 4 [He made him fatherless]. (TA in art. ايم.) See an ex. in art. ايم, conj. 2.
